page: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-960GB-4xH100 is a GPU-accelerated server designed for high-performance computing and machine learning workloads. It features 48 physical Intel Xeon 8462Y+ cores on the x86_64 architecture, running at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 960.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 20.0 GB per core. Acceleration is driven by four NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs, delivering a combined 320 GB of VRAM. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and virtualization is managed via the KVM hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in memory bandwidth and data decompression. The memory bandwidth for small cached blocks reaches 7649.82 GB/sec, while large-block read and write speeds are recorded at 316.04 GB/sec and 135.84 GB/sec respectively. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression achieves a top-tier throughput of 615.79 GB/sec. For AI workloads, LLM inference using a small 135M model yields 10308.98 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 377.14 tokens/sec for text generation. Passmark CPU testing registers a top-tier score of 85426.66, with strong performance in database operations and cryptography.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server represents a premium tier optimized for resource density, trading lower-cost general-purpose compute for dense GPU acceleration and massive memory capacity. The lack of integrated local storage requires external storage provisioning, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This configuration is highly suited for large-scale deep learning, LLM inference, parallel scientific simulations, and memory-intensive data science pipelines.
description: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-960GB-4xH100 is a high-density, GPU-accelerated server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 physical Intel Xeon 8462Y+ cores running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 960.0 GB of system memory. The primary performance driver is a quad-GPU array consisting of four NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs with 320 GB of total VRAM. This server does not include local storage, requiring external storage solutions. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory bandwidth of 7649.82 GB/sec for cached blocks and exceptional multi-threaded decompression speeds. LLM inference benchmarks show top-tier performance, processing 10308.98 tokens/sec on a 135M model. This hardware profile is qualitatively positioned as a premium, resource-dense solution for workloads that demand massive parallel processing and high memory capacity, such as deep learning, large-scale AI inference, and complex scientific simulations.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-960GB-4xH100 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 48 Intel Xeon cores, 960 GB RAM, and four NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs with 320 GB VRAM, optimized for deep learning and HPC.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-960GB-4xH100 features 48 Intel Xeon cores, 960 GB RAM, and four NVIDIA H100 GPUs for high-performance AI and deep learning.
tagline: A high-density accelerator platform combining quad Hopper GPUs with massive system memory for demanding parallel computing and AI workloads.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs with 320 GB total VRAM
48 physical Intel Xeon 8462Y+ cores with 960 GB RAM
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression performance
Exceptional LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation
Best suited for deep learning, AI training, and scientific computing
